title: What Does a Fractional CMO Do? The Whole Function | Daniel Fox
meta_description: What a fractional CMO does: runs the entire marketing function — strategy, content, SEO, AI-search visibility, paid, capture, nurture, creative, and measurement — part-time, with one number to hold.

mainEntity.name: How is this different from an agency?

acceptedAnswer.text: An agency executes the tasks you assign and reports activity. A fractional CMO answers for the outcome — sets the strategy, directs the agencies and freelancers you already have, and stands behind the number. You stop managing marketing; I run it.

mainEntity.name: How is it different from hiring a full-time CMO?

acceptedAnswer.text: Same caliber of leader, without the full-time salary, the equity, or the six-month search and ramp. Ownership-level responsibility without ownership-level equity — starting in weeks, not quarters.
I work with established companies, typically doing $3–12M. Engagements are scoped and sized to your stage. If the first question is “what's the cheapest option,” it's probably not the fit yet.

The diagnosis is quick — you'll know where customers really come from in the first weeks. Building and optimizing demand compounds over a considered-purchase cycle. I set a baseline up front, so what you see is genuinely new, not a number I backed into.

mainEntity.name: Will you work with my current team and agency?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — I lead them. Most companies already have some marketing in motion. I run and direct it, cut what isn't working, and add what's missing.
Strategy through pipeline: positioning, channels, content and search, paid, email, creative, and the measurement to prove it. One leader, accountable for the result.

@graph.serviceType: Fractional B2C CMO / fractional marketing leadership

audience.name: Established, profitable companies with considered, high-ticket purchases
